title: Stative vs dynamic verbs
explanation:
Stative vs dynamic verbs

Dynamic verbs describe actions and can use continuous forms: run, eat, play, work

Stative verbs describe states, feelings, possession, senses — usually NOT continuous: know, believe, love, hate, need, want, belong, seem

  • I know the answer. (NOT I am knowing)
  • I am thinking about it. (= considering — dynamic use)

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: She ____ him. They've been married for years. | *loves | is loving | love
Listen! Someone ____ at the door. | *is knocking | knocks | knock
I ____ this film. It's boring. | *don't like | am not liking | not like
The soup ____ delicious. | *smells | is smelling | smell
We [*are having|have|having] dinner at the moment.
I [*don't understand|am not understanding|not understand] this exercise.
